mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, PKC, and KIT to reduce AML cell proliferation and survival
additional_notes: Also known as PKC412; approved for FLT3-mutated AML; strong activity in FLT3-ITD–positive lines such as MOLM-13 and MV4-11

mechanism: Selectively inhibits FLT3 and AXL tyrosine kinases, blocking FLT3-ITD–driven signaling
additional_notes: Also known as ASP2215; approved for relapsed/refractory FLT3-mutated AML; highly potent in MOLM-13

mechanism: Potent and selective inhibition of FLT3, especially FLT3-ITD, leading to apoptosis of dependent AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as AC220; benchmark FLT3 inhibitor in FLT3-ITD AML models including MOLM-13 and MV4-11

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, RAF kinases, VEGFR, and PDGFR, disrupting proliferative and survival signaling
additional_notes: Off-label use in FLT3-ITD–positive AML; broad kinase inhibition with strong preclinical data in MOLM-13 and MV4-11

mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL, SRC family kinases, KIT, and other kinases, impairing oncogenic signaling in AML
additional_notes: Used clinically in CML/ALL; has reported activity in various AML cell lines

mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL, KIT, and PDGFR to block signaling in kinase-driven leukemias
additional_notes: Most effective in KIT- or PDGFR-driven AML subsets; less active in FLT3-driven lines like MOLM-13 but still profiled in vitro

mechanism: Selectively binds and inhibits BCL2, promoting mitochondrial apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as ABT-199; approved for AML in combination regimens; MOLM-13 often shows low-nM sensitivity

mechanism: Blocks the MDM2–p53 interaction, stabilizing and activating p53 to induce apoptosis in p53–wild-type AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as RG7388; efficacy depends on wild-type p53 status (e.g., MOLM-13 if p53 wt)

mechanism: Inhibits multiple histone deacetylases, altering chromatin structure and gene expression to induce apoptosis and differentiation
additional_notes: Also known as LBH589; epigenetic modifier; reported synergy with FLT3 and BCL2 inhibitors in FLT3-ITD AML

mechanism: Inhibits class I/II histone deacetylases, leading to hyperacetylation of histones and non-histone proteins and impaired AML cell growth
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA; can induce differentiation and apoptosis in AML cells

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and inhibits DNMTs, causing hypomethylation and reactivation of silenced genes in AML cells
additional_notes: Hypomethylating agent approved for AML/MDS; unstable in solution, often requires frequent medium/drug refresh

mechanism: Incorporates into RNA and DNA and inhibits DNMTs, leading to DNA hypomethylation and altered gene expression
additional_notes: Standard-of-care hypomethylating agent in AML; stability issues in vitro require careful handling

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2 kinases, suppressing aberrant JAK/STAT signaling in AML cells with pathway activation
additional_notes: Used clinically in myelofibrosis and PV; investigated in JAK/STAT-driven AML or cytokine-dependent growth

mechanism: Inhibits mTORC1, reducing protein synthesis and cell growth downstream of PI3K/AKT/mTOR signaling
additional_notes: Also known as RAD001; targets a pathway frequently hyperactive in AML and may sensitize to other agents

mechanism: Binds FKBP12 to inhibit mTOR activity, decreasing translation and cell growth in AML cells
additional_notes: Pharmacologically similar to other rapalogs; preclinical data support use in AML models

mechanism: Reversibly inhibits the 26S proteasome, causing accumulation of misfolded proteins and inhibition of NF-κB signaling
additional_notes: Approved for multiple myeloma; induces apoptosis in many AML cell lines

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the chymotrypsin-like activity of the 20S proteasome, disrupting protein homeostasis and inducing apoptosis
additional_notes: Second-generation proteasome inhibitor; potent activity in hematologic malignancies including AML models

mechanism: Raises lysosomal pH and blocks autophagosome-lysosome fusion, inhibiting autophagy and stressing AML cells
additional_notes: Autophagy inhibitor; hydroxychloroquine is a close analog; often used in combination with FLT3 or BCL2 inhibitors in AML studies

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial complex I and activates AMPK, altering cellular energy metabolism and reducing AML cell proliferation
additional_notes: Requires relatively high in vitro concentrations for AML effects; studied for impact on leukemia stem cells and metabolic vulnerability

mechanism: Binds cereblon within the CRL4 E3 ligase complex, modulating degradation of substrates and altering cytokine production and immune/microenvironment interactions
additional_notes: Lenalidomide is a close analog with similar mechanism; approved for other hematologic cancers; AML effects often microenvironment- and immune-mediated with modest direct cytotoxicity
